observations.summary: Stale red-phase headers persist for a 3rd story (3.4, 3.7, 3.8): prepare-tests creates them, implement-story un-skips tests without updating them, and a downstream review step must fix them; the 3.7 update_step to review-tests is now working as a guard (0 stale markers found in scope) but the root cause persists and 2 out-of-scope stale headers from Story 1.3 remain unresolved.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-code: patch #3 'Stale TDD RED PHASE comments in 3 test files — headers say tests are skipped but all tests are active. Fixed: update comments to GREEN PHASE' (story file _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-8-track-per-user-llm-spend.md:483). Journal step_end for review-tests: '0 stale markers found in Story 3.8 test files. All 3 test file headers correctly say TDD GREEN PHASE' and 'The stale markers were already remediated during the Story 3.8 code review' (test-review-validation-report-3-8.md:70-92). Out-of-scope: 'Two Story 1.3 test files have stale RED PHASE headers — apps/web/src/actions/repo-connection.actions.spec.ts:9, apps/web/src/lib/auth.credential.spec.ts:9' (test-review-validation-report-3-8.md:98-105). ATDD checklist line 223 confirms prepare-tests created the red-phase headers. Ledger records 3.7 update_step to review-tests (applied 2026-07-05T08:14:39.210Z).

observations.summary: The implement-story step did not proactively apply the select projection pattern on prisma.costRecord.create — a pattern documented in project-context.md and loaded by the bmad-dev-story skill — forcing the review-nfrs step to catch it; 2nd occurrence after Story 2.2 where the same class required 6 NFR fixes.
observations.evidence: Story file NFR Audit section: '[NFR-Audit][LOW] Missing select projection on prisma.costRecord.create — the create call returns all 8 columns but none are read. Inconsistent with the select: { id: true } pattern used on prisma.turn.create and prisma.conversation.update in the same code path' (_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-8-track-per-user-llm-spend.md:514). Journal step_end for review-nfrs lists this as finding #2. bmad-dev-story SKILL.md line 57 confirms the skill loads project-context.md as a persistent fact. Ledger records first occurrence for Story 2.2 (distinctRuns: 1) where 6 NFR fixes were needed including missing select projection.

observations.summary: The prepare-tests step applied TDD red-phase scaffolding (stub files, test seams, existing-file modifications) but did not update the story file to reflect the completed scaffolding tasks, forcing validate-story-2 to fix 7 tasks presented as 'to do' that were already done.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for validate-story-2: 'Story contradicted the codebase — red-phase scaffolding not reflected (DP-2). The story was written before ATDD red-phase scaffolding was applied, then never updated. Tasks 2, 3, 4, 5.1, 9.1, 9.2, and 9.3 were presented as to do but were already done (test seams + stubs scaffolded). A dev following the story literally would re-add CostTrackingModule to StreamingModule (already imported), re-inject CostTrackingService (already the 5th constructor arg), and re-create mockCostTracking + makeResultMessage (already scaffolded).' ATDD checklist lines 225-237 confirm prepare-tests created stub files (cost-tracking.service.ts, cost-tracking.module.ts) and modified existing files (agent.service.ts, streaming.module.ts). No trace exists for this run to confirm whether prepare-tests considered updating the story file.
observations.hypothesis: The prepare-tests step's prompt ('Create tests') does not instruct it to update the story file after applying scaffolding, so the story drifts from the codebase state until validate-story-2 catches the contradiction; this likely recurs on any story where prepare-tests creates stub files for TDD red-phase compilation.
observations.nextStep: Check prior stories' validate-story-2 journal excerpts (via node scripts/pipeline/journal.mjs story <id>) for the same 'story contradicted codebase — red-phase scaffolding not reflected' pattern; if it recurs, confirm the prepare-tests step's prompt should include updating the story file to mark scaffolded tasks as done. If traces become available for future runs, widen into traces/<runId>/prepare-tests.json with --grep 'story' or --grep 'scaffold' to see whether the agent considered updating the story file.

observations.summary: The prepare-tests step created NFR-S1 regression guards that are incomplete for 3 of 5 executeCommand call sites — they assert the command string contains no credentials but do not assert the env arg is empty, unlike the clone() test which shows the correct pattern; a future change adding credentials via the env arg would not be caught.
observations.evidence: Story file NFR Audit section: '[NFR-Audit][MEDIUM] NFR-S1 regression guard incomplete for 3 of 5 executeCommand call sites — injectGitConfig(), commit(), and listSkills() tests assert the command string (arg 0) contains no credential env-var names, but do NOT assert the executeCommand env arg (arg 2) is undefined. The clone() test (lines 99-110) shows the correct pattern: expect(callArgs[2]).toBeUndefined()' (_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-8-track-per-user-llm-spend.md:512). Journal step_end for review-nfrs lists this as finding #1 (MEDIUM). No trace exists for this run to confirm whether prepare-tests considered the env-arg assertion for all call sites.
observations.hypothesis: The prepare-tests step does not apply a consistent regression-guard pattern across all executeCommand call sites — it checks the env arg for clone() but not for the other 3 call sites, suggesting the agent treated each call site independently rather than deriving a uniform guard template; this may recur on future stories that create regression guards for multiple call sites of the same method.
observations.nextStep: Check prior stories' NFR-S1 or security regression guard tests (e.g., sandbox.service.nfr-s1.spec.ts from earlier stories, or similar regression-guard test files) for the same inconsistency — guards that check command strings but not env args. If traces become available for future runs, widen into traces/<runId>/prepare-tests.json with --grep 'executeCommand' or --grep 'env' to see whether the agent considered the env-arg assertion for all call sites.

amendments.prompt: Develop story autonomously, completing every task listed in the story spec before finishing. Apply non-functional patterns documented in project-context.md proactively — do not wait for the NFR review to catch them. As each task makes a skipped test pass, remove its skip marker — the story is not complete while skipped tests remain for done tasks. For any decision that arises, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md before escalating: if a rule covers it, decide and continue. HALT only for decisions no rule covers.
amendments.rationale: The implement-story step has skipped proactive NFR hardening in 2 stories (2.2, 3.8), forcing the review-nfrs step to catch patterns documented in project-context.md that the dev-story skill already loads; explicitly directing the agent to apply them proactively is a low-risk prompt tuning that reduces rework.
